title: Line Graph: The graph below shows the production of three forest industry products in a Europe...
description: The graph below shows the production of three forest industry products in a European country, namely timber, pulp, and paper from 1980 to 1996. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make a comparison where relevant.
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Forest Industry Production (1980–1996)
1\. Giải thích đề

Biểu đồ đường cho thấy sản lượng (triệu tấn) của ba ngành: pulp, timber và paper trong giai đoạn 1980–1996.

→ Dạng: line graph (time \+ production)

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

The line graph illustrates the production of pulp, timber and paper in the forest industry between 1980 and 1996, measured in million tonnes.

3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Nhìn chung, paper và timber có xu hướng tăng, trong khi pulp dao động
  • Paper tăng mạnh nhất và kết thúc cao nhất
  • Pulp đạt đỉnh sớm rồi giảm nhẹ về cuối
4\. Cách chia BODY

→ Chia theo trend: tăng đều vs dao động

BODY 1 – Paper & Timber (xu hướng tăng)
  • Paper:

→ tăng liên tục từ khoảng 4 (1980) lên 12 (1996)

→ có một số dao động nhẹ nhưng nhìn chung là tăng mạnh và ổn định

  • Timber:

→ giảm mạnh ban đầu (1980–1982)

→ sau đó tăng đều từ khoảng 3 lên gần 10 (1996)

→ Key idea:

  • Cả hai ngành đều tăng trưởng dài hạn, đặc biệt paper là nhanh nhất
BODY 2 – Pulp (dao động)
  • Pulp:

→ tăng từ \~4.5 lên gần 9.5 (1984)

→ sau đó giảm dần và dao động quanh mức 7–8

→ kết thúc khoảng 7.5 (1996)

→ Key idea:

  • Pulp có xu hướng biến động, không tăng rõ rệt như hai ngành còn lại
5\. Từ vựng & cấu trúc ăn điểm
Trend verbs:
  • increase steadily
  • fluctuate
  • peak at
  • decline slightly
Useful structures:
  • showed an overall upward trend
  • reached a peak in
  • experienced fluctuations
  • ended the period at
So sánh:
  • higher than
  • in contrast
  • compared to
Ví dụ câu:
  • Paper production showed a steady increase and finished as the highest figure.
  • Timber recovered after an initial drop and rose consistently thereafter.
  • In contrast, pulp fluctuated and remained relatively stable overall.

The line graph illustrates the production of pulp, timber and paper in a forest industry between 1980 and 1996, measured in million tonnes.

Overall, paper production increased steadily and became the highest by the end of the period. Timber also showed a general upward trend despite an early decline, while pulp fluctuated and finished slightly below its mid-period peak.

In 1980, timber was the leading product at around 5.5 million tonnes, followed by pulp at approximately 4.5 million tonnes and paper at just under 4 million tonnes. However, timber output dropped sharply to about 3 million tonnes in 1982 before recovering to nearly 7 million tonnes by 1984\. After that, it rose gradually, reaching around 9.5 million tonnes in 1996\.

Pulp production increased significantly in the early years, climbing from roughly 4.5 million tonnes in 1980 to a peak of about 9.5 million tonnes in 1984\. It then declined and fluctuated between 6.5 and 8 million tonnes for the remainder of the period, ending at approximately 7.5 million tonnes.

By contrast, paper production followed a consistent upward trend throughout. It rose from about 3.7 million tonnes in 1980 to around 7.5 million tonnes in 1990, then continued to grow, overtaking both pulp and timber after 1992 and peaking at approximately 12 million tonnes in 1996\.
